reasoning: The first round of the Chilean presidential election will occur in November 2025. Recent polling data from October 2025 indicates that Johannes Kaiser (leader of the National Libertarian Party) is polling in fourth place at approximately 12%. He is trailing significantly behind Jeannette Jara (~29-30%), José Antonio Kast (~23%), and Evelyn Matthei (~14-16%). While early 2025 polls showed him surging into a tie for the lead, that momentum has completely faded over the following months. He remains a fractured far-right alternative to Kast, splitting the vote but remaining far from contention for the top spot. Winning the *most* valid votes outright in the first round (requiring >23-30% of the vote based on current leading contenders) is practically impossible for Kaiser at this point given that the election is in less than one month. A 2% chance perfectly reflects this highly unlikely tail event.
